message:
test(readme): cover full Window Operations matrix (fd90.199 / br-9fk38)

Extends readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the full
Window Operations matrix documented in README lines 489-496. Previously
only 4 methods were tested; now exercises 51 calls across 8 method
groups:

Series Rolling (10): sum, mean, min, max, std, var, count, median,
  quantile, apply (with Fn(&[f64]) -> f64 closure)
Series Expanding (8): sum, mean, min, max, std, var, median, apply
Series EWM (3): mean, std, var
Series Resample (7): sum, mean, count, min, max, first, last
  (on a datetime-indexed Series via Series::from_values with date
  IndexLabels)
DataFrame Rolling (9): sum, mean, min, max, std, var, count, median,
  quantile
DataFrame Expanding (7): sum, mean, min, max, std, var, median
DataFrame EWM (3): mean, std, var
DataFrame Resample (5): sum, mean, count, min, max

Defect found: README claims DataFrameResample supports first/last, but
the impl at fp-frame line 8825 only has 5 methods (sum/mean/count/min/
max). Series Resample (line 8244) does have first/last, so the asymmetry
is the bug. Filed separately as fd90.200; this commit leaves a comment
in the test pointing to that bead.

Verified: 34 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-9fk38","title":"fd90.199: Cover full Window Operations matrix in integration test","description":"fd90.199: Extend readme_window_operations to cover the full Window Operations matrix at README lines 489-496.

The README enumerates 28 distinct window operations (10 Rolling Series + 9 Rolling DataFrame + 8 Expanding Series + 7 Expanding DataFrame + 3 EWM Series + 3 EWM DataFrame + 7 Resample Series + 7 Resample DataFrame; many overlap, ~28 unique cells). Today the test exercises only 4 (rolling.mean, rolling.std, expanding.max, ewm.mean) — all on Series, none on DataFrame, and resample is uncovered entirely.

Scope (extend existing test or add new):
- Series Rolling: sum, mean, min, max, std, var, count, median, quantile, apply
- Series Expanding: sum, mean, min, max, std, var, median, apply
- Series EWM: mean, std, var
- Series Resample: sum, mean, count, min, max, first, last
- DataFrame Rolling: sum, mean, min, max, std, var, count, median, quantile
- DataFrame Expanding: sum, mean, min, max, std, var, median
- DataFrame EWM: mean, std, var
- DataFrame Resample: sum, mean, count, min, max, first, last

Each call invoked once on a small numeric Series/DataFrame; assertion: returned Series/DataFrame has same length as input (or expected resampled length).

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (extend existing or add ~80 lines).
Time estimate: 35-50min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:33:18.102491343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:36:21.706024409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:36:21.703912027Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the full Window Operations matrix at README lines 489-496. Now exercises Series Rolling (10 methods incl. apply with Fn(&[f64])->f64), Series Expanding (8 methods), Series EWM (3), Series Resample (7 methods on a datetime-indexed Series), DataFrame Rolling (9), DataFrame Expanding (7), DataFrame EWM (3), DataFrame Resample (5 — first/last not yet implemented per fd90.200). Found a real defect: README claims DataFrame Resample supports first/last but DataFrameResample impl is missing those methods. Filing fd90.200 separately. All 34 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9fm8","title":"Add strict series head zero and negative-n packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T04:20:17.071417723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T04:22:32.391047786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T04:22:32.388964704Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-9fsqn","title":"fd90.129: TypeError + GroupByError not re-exported from frankenpandas (other 6 of 8 documented errors are)","description":"fd90.74-128 README audit. Error Architecture table at lines 821-830 documents 8 error types as part of the 'frankenpandas error surface' that users will pattern-match. The example at line 835 imports them via 'use frankenpandas::{IoError, FrameError, ExprError};' but two of the 8 documented error types aren't re-exported:

- TypeError (fp-types) — defined at crates/fp-types/src/lib.rs:301, pub
- GroupByError (fp-groupby) — defined at crates/fp-groupby/src/lib.rs:91, pub

Verified at crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs: searches for 'TypeError' and 'GroupByError' return nothing in the re-export section. A user reading the error architecture and trying to pattern-match would hit:
  error[E0432]: unresolved import 'frankenpandas::TypeError'

The other 6 error types (ColumnError, IndexError, FrameError, ExprError, JoinError, IoError) ARE re-exported. fd90.121/125/126/127/128 set the precedent that the frankenpandas top-level surface should match the README's documented surface.

Fix: re-export TypeError from fp_types and GroupByError from fp_groupby at the top level of frankenpandas. Errors don't need to be in the prelude (matches existing pattern — IoError etc are top-level only), but they do need to be reachable via 'use frankenpandas::{TypeError, GroupByError}'. Pure re-export expansion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:03:59.493000716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:06:35.064296335Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:06:35.061577002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-kw5q","title":"[LOW] docs: no per-crate README.md — 11 fp-* crates on crates.io will show the workspace README (or nothing)","description":"Per-crate README audit.

**Observation.** `find crates -maxdepth 2 -name README.md` → zero matches. The workspace has 12 crates; none have a per-crate README. Users landing on crates.io for a specific fp-* crate (e.g. someone arriving at `crates.io/crates/fp-frame` from a search result) see no crate-specific documentation.

**What crates.io does without a per-crate README.** It falls back to the workspace-root README if `[package].readme = "../../README.md"` is set, OR shows nothing if the path is wrong. The workspace-root README.md is ~70 KB and workspace-oriented ("FrankenPandas: clean-room Rust reimplementation of pandas"). A user arriving at fp-frame specifically sees text about all 12 crates rather than just fp-frame.

**When it matters.** Once br-4clx ships release, each fp-* crate becomes independently discoverable on crates.io. A user searching "dataframe rust" who lands on fp-frame wants to know what fp-frame does, what it requires from other crates in the workspace (fp-columnar, fp-index, fp-runtime, fp-types), and how to use it standalone vs via the frankenpandas umbrella. Workspace README doesn't answer any of that.

**Severity.** LOW — cosmetic / discoverability. Honest calibration: release-day polish, not a blocker. br-h8a8 (crates.io metadata) partially addresses via per-crate descriptions; per-crate README is the next tier of discoverability polish.

**Fix shape.** Per-crate `crates/fp-XXX/README.md`:

```markdown
# fp-frame

DataFrame and Series with pandas-API parity and AACE index alignment.

Core crate of the [frankenpandas](https://github.com/<org>/frankenpandas)
workspace. Most users should depend on the umbrella `frankenpandas` crate
rather than fp-frame directly.

## When to use fp-frame directly

- Building a pandas-compatible Rust library that doesn't need fp-io's
  CSV/Parquet/Excel parsers.
- Embedding DataFrame/Series primitives in a higher-level crate.

## Dependencies within the workspace

- `fp-types` for Scalar, DType, NullKind.
- `fp-columnar` for Column / ValidityMask.
- `fp-index` for Index / MultiIndex.
- `fp-runtime` for RuntimePolicy / EvidenceLedger.

## Example

```rust
use fp_frame::DataFrame;
use fp_columnar::Column;
use fp_index::Index;

let df = DataFrame::from_dict(...)?;
let grouped = df.groupby(&["region"])?.sum()?;
```

See the [workspace README](https://github.com/<org>/frankenpandas) for
the full API tour and conformance status.
```

Per-crate descriptions vary — fp-io gets an "IO formats supported" section, fp-runtime documents the asupersync feature flag, etc.

Blast radius: 12 new README.md files (~500-800 lines total). Each crate's Cargo.toml needs `readme = "README.md"` (default, but set explicitly if the workspace-level `[workspace.package].readme = "README.md"` is configured — that would point every crate at the workspace README). Zero source changes. Zero CI changes.

Prerequisites:
- br-h8a8 (crates.io metadata) — this bead is the natural continuation of h8a8's per-crate description work.
- br-4clx (release workflow) — landing per-crate READMEs before first publish saves a 0.1.1 discoverability-bump.

**Related:**
- br-h8a8 (Cargo.toml description/keywords/categories) — same release-day polish wave.
- br-7cfm (rustdoc + panic contract) — per-crate README links to `docs.rs/fp-X` for the full API; rustdoc renders the full reference; README is the landing page.
- br-ddox (doctests in CI) — per-crate README examples should be doctests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:09:51.542117527Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:18.242696Z","close_reason":"per-crate README.md already shipped on main across all 12 crates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discoverability","docs","release","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-kzmxd","title":"fd90.200: Implement DataFrameResample::first/last to match README","description":"fd90.200: Add first()/last() to DataFrameResample to match README claim.

The README at line 494 enumerates Resample methods including first and last. Series Resample (impl Resample at fp-frame line 8244) has both. DataFrameResample (impl at line 8825) is missing them — exposed during fd90.199 integration test write.

Two ways to honor the README:
1. Implement DataFrameResample::first() and ::last() that delegate to per-column Resample::first()/::last() via column_as_series, mirroring the existing DataFrameResample::sum/mean/count/min/max pattern.
2. Update the README to remove first/last from the Resample row and document the asymmetry in DISCREPANCIES.md.

Option 1 is the right move — Series Resample already has the implementation, so DataFrameResample is just glue. Plus it removes the README defect at the source rather than papering over it.

Files: crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs (impl DataFrameResample around line 8825), crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (re-enable the two skipped calls in readme_window_operations).
